presume.definition: With infinitive object: to be so presumptuous as (to do something) without proper authority or permission.

prevail.definition: To be superior in strength, dominance, influence or frequency; to have or gain the advantage over others; to have the upper hand; to outnumber others.

prevalence.definition: The quality or condition of being prevalent; wide extension or spread.

prevention.definition: The act of preventing or hindering; obstruction of action, access, or approach; thwarting.

prey.definition: Anything, as goods, etc., taken or got by violence; anything taken by force from an enemy in war

privatization.definition: The transfer of a company or organization from government to private ownership and control.

privilege.definition: (ecclesiastical law) An exemption from certain laws granted by the Pope.

probe.definition: Any of various medical instruments used to explore wounds, organs, etc.

problematic.definition: (chiefly in the plural) A problem or difficulty in a particular field of study.

proceeding.definition: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

proceeds.definition: To move, pass, or go forward or onward; to advance; to carry on

processing.definition: To perform a particular process on a thing.

processor.definition: A person or institution who processes things (foods, photos, applications, etc.).

productive.definition: Capable of producing something, especially in abundance; fertile.

productivity.definition: The state of being productive, fertile or efficient

profound.definition: The deep; the sea; the ocean.

projection.definition: Something which projects, protrudes, juts out, sticks out, or stands out.

prominent.definition: Standing out, or projecting; jutting; protuberant

pronounced.definition: To declare formally, officially or ceremoniously.

propaganda.definition: A concerted set of messages aimed at influencing the opinions or behavior of large numbers of people.

proposition.definition: The act of offering (an idea) for consideration.

prosecution.definition: The act of prosecuting a scheme or endeavor.

prospective.definition: The scene before or around, in time or in space; view; prospect.

prosperity.definition: The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune

protocol.definition: The minutes, or official record, of a negotiation or transaction; especially a document drawn up officially which forms the legal basis for subsequent agreements based on it.

province.definition: A region of the earth or of a continent; a district or country.

provincial.definition: A person belonging to a province; one who is provincial.

provision.definition: An item of goods or supplies, especially food, obtained for future use.

provoke.definition: To cause someone to become annoyed or angry.

pulse.definition: A normally regular beat felt when arteries are depressed, caused by the pumping action of the heart.

pump.definition: A device for moving or compressing a liquid or gas.

punch.definition: A hit or strike with one's fist.

query.definition: A question, an inquiry (US), an enquiry (UK).

quest.definition: A journey or effort in pursuit of a goal (often lengthy, ambitious, or fervent); a mission.

quota.definition: A proportional part or share; the share or proportion assigned to each in a division.

radar.definition: A method of detecting distant objects and determining their position, velocity, or other characteristics by analysis of sent radio waves (usually microwaves) reflected from their surfaces

radical.definition: (historical: 19th-century Britain) A member of the most progressive wing of the Liberal Party; someone favouring social reform (but generally stopping short of socialism).

raid.definition: A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.

rally.definition: A public gathering or mass meeting that is not mainly a protest and is organized to inspire enthusiasm for a cause.

ranking.definition: To place abreast, or in a line.

ratio.definition: A number representing a comparison between two named things.

rational.definition: A rational number: a number that can be expressed as the quotient of two integers.

ray.definition: A beam of light or radiation.

readily.definition: Without unwillingness or hesitation; showing readiness.

realm.definition: An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined.

rear.definition: To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.

reasoning.definition: To deduce or come to a conclusion by being rational

reassure.definition: To assure anew; to restore confidence to; to free from fear or self-doubt.

rebel.definition: A person who resists an established authority, often violently

rebellion.definition: Armed resistance to an established government or ruler.

reconstruction.definition: A thing that has been reconstructed or restored to an earlier state.

recruitment.definition: The process or art of finding candidates for a post in an organization, or recruits for the armed forces.

referendum.definition: A direct popular vote on a proposed law or constitutional amendment. The adposition on is usually used before the related subject of the vote.

reflection.definition: The act of reflecting or the state of being reflected.

reform.definition: The change of something that is defective, broken, inefficient or otherwise negative, in order to correct or improve it

refuge.definition: A state of safety, protection or shelter.

regain.definition: To get back; to recover possession of.

regulator.definition: A device that controls or limits something.

rehabilitation.definition: The process of rehabilitating somebody or something.

relevance.definition: The property or state of being relevant or pertinent.

reliability.definition: The quality of being reliable, dependable or trustworthy.

remainder.definition: A part or parts remaining after some has/have been removed.

remains.definition: (chiefly in the plural) That which is left; relic; remainder.

removal.definition: The process of moving, or the fact of being removed.

render.definition: Stucco or plaster applied to walls (mostly to outside masonry walls).

renew.definition: To make (something) new again; to restore to freshness or original condition.

replacement.definition: A person or thing that takes the place of another; a substitute.

reportedly.definition: According to reports or rumors; supposedly.

reproduce.definition: To produce an image or copy of.

reproduction.definition: The act of reproducing new individuals biologically.

republic.definition: A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives, rather than with a monarch or emperor; a country with no monarchy.

resemble.definition: To be like or similar to (something); to represent as similar.

reside.definition: To dwell permanently or for a considerable time; to have a settled abode for a time; to remain for a long time.

residence.definition: The place where one lives; one's home.
